channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOGE's plans to offload government buildings supported by former GSA official
item.description: Former GSA Administrator Emily Murphy told Fox News Digital that the GSA will “rightsize its portfolio" by selling or leasing unused government buildings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration ramps up rhetoric targeting the courts amid mounting legal setbacks
item.description: President Donald Trump’s supporters are echoing some of the rhetoric and actions that elsewhere have preceded attacks on the judiciary as the courts deliver a series of setbacks to his dramatic attempt to change the federal government without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: VA denies policy change after internal memo rescinds transgender veterans’ guidance
item.description: In an internal VA memo seen by NPR, the VA says it's rescinding a directive that contains detailed guidance on the kind of care transgender veterans can receive at VA facilities.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota National Guard members receive email instructing those with gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate
item.description: The email to National Guard members lays out a timeline for service members who experience gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate from the military.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen say a series of airstrikes have hit the capital, Sanaa, on Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked just hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs a bill funding the government for 6 months, avoiding a shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed into law legislation funding the government through the end of September, ending the threat of a partial government shutdown and capping off a struggle in Congress that deeply divided Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China's US influence could face crackdown under slate of new bills
item.description: U.S. Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., rolled out several bills aimed at countering Chinese influence and ownership in the U.S. as tensions grow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges don't intervene after the Trump administration says it's stopped destroying USAID records
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal judges in two separate lawsuits refused Friday to order the Trump administration to not destroy U.S. Agency for International Development records after it said it has disposed of only old or unneeded documents and is no longer destroying records anyway.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oz pledges to fight health care fraud but makes no commitments on Medicaid funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dr. Mehmet Oz promised senators on Friday to fight health care fraud and push to make Americans healthier if he becomes the next leader of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian reporters grill Rubio over Trump's 51st state talk
item.description: Rubio said Trump’s stance on Canada joining the U.S. came following a dinner conversation with former Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Proposals to cut down on bad driving cross political lanes in Minnesota session
item.description: A variety of bills that aim to make Minnesota’s roads less treacherous are rolling their way through the legislative process. It could wind up as one area of bipartisan consensus in a session where that is a paramount.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Trump's Irish golf course damaged by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: The incident comes less than a week after pro-Palestinian protestors dug up some of the Trump Turnberry greens in Scotland.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer backing budget bill a 'slap in the face': AOC
item.description: She called the Senate minority leader's intent to vote for a GOP-backed spending bill a "slap in the face.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats struggling with unified strategy to oppose Trump, Musk
item.description: Schumer's decision to vote for a Republican-sponsored budget bill called “a huge slap in the face" for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Proposal seeks to safeguard Nevada bus drivers from passenger assaults
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would require public transit companies to install safety barriers and panic buttons amid an uptick in driver assaults. Democratic Assem.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Overpaid by Social Security? You’ll now owe 100% of the repayment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – If you accidentally got overpaid through your Social Security benefits, you’ll have to pay 100% of it back starting in late March, the agency announced on Monday. The overpayment withholding rate was previously 10%.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DUI punishments higher under 2 bills introduced Monday in Nevada Senate
item.description: Republicans are proposing more severe punishment for driving under the influence with two new bills introduced Monday in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
